title: PB Inspired Daily System — Letter Bin
summary: A wall-mounted mail sorter that turns an 11x14 picture frame into two angled letter pockets. A clever scrap-plywood build with a printable template.
description:
This is a knock-off of the Pottery Barn 'Daily System' letter bin: a store-bought 11x14 picture frame becomes the face of a shallow two-pocket mail sorter, with angled 1/4" plywood sides and fronts glued inside the frame to hold letters and papers. It's a fast, clever project that leans on the frame to do the finished-edge work for you.

The build hinges on a printable template. Print it in landscape, scaled to fit the media, cut it out, and trace it onto 1/4" plywood to get the two angled side pieces (a) — the angle is what tips the pockets forward so mail is visible and easy to grab. Cut them with a jigsaw or circular saw. The top bin front (c) gets two small 1/4" x 1" notches cut out; check the source template for their exact placement.

The assembly trick is to keep the frame's glass in place while you glue the sides to the inside of the frame — it holds everything at the right spacing for a proper fit — but avoid gluing to the glass itself. Once the sides are set, add the two bin fronts. Paint or finish the plywood to match your frame. It's a great small gift and an easy way to tame the pile of mail by the door.

tools.note: Cuts the angled template sides and the notches.

materials.name: Picture frame, 11 in x 14 in
materials.note: Forms the face; keep the glass in for spacing during assembly.

cutList.note: Traced from the printable template — the angle sets the pocket rake.
Notch two 1/4" x 1" rectangles per the template.

steps.title: Print and prepare the template
steps.body: Print out the template — select landscape orientation and 'scale to fit media' in your print options so the angles come out at the right size.

steps.title: Trace and cut the sides
steps.body: Cut out the template and trace it onto the 1/4" plywood. Cut the two side pieces (a) with a jigsaw or circular saw. These angled sides are what tip the pockets forward.

steps.title: Notch the top bin front
steps.body: Notch out two 1/4" wide x 1" high rectangles from the top bin front (c), positioned per the template.

steps.title: Glue the sides into the frame and finish
steps.body: Glue the sides (a) to the inside of the frame. Keep the glass in place while you do this — it holds everything at the right spacing for a proper fit — but avoid gluing to the glass. Once the sides are set, add the two bin fronts (b) and (c). Sand and paint or finish to match the frame, then mount it to the wall.

images.alt: Wall-mounted letter bin made from a picture frame with angled pockets
